claims.statement: Local causal influence does not imply cross-domain certification authority.

failure_condition.description: A routing-only or persistent internal state creates or strengthens an EvidenceLink without a declared grounding rule and observation artifact.

claims.notes: ADOPTED records program adoption, not empirical confirmation. No authority-specific historical case is linked yet; the current implementation covers only part of the possible certification domains.

claims.statement: Within the versioned scenario-to-DynamicsEngine execution path, a processed temporal envelope preserves occurred_at <= available_at <= processed_at, processing time is monotonic, processed_at does not exceed the run's final_sim_time, and the engine path assigns the processing timestamp.

failure_condition.description: A delivery is processed with an inverted timestamp order, processed_at regresses or exceeds final_sim_time, or an input-supplied processed_at is accepted as the engine processing stamp.

claims.notes: This is a protocol and trace integrity result, not evidence for a human subjective-time law or a capability-secure Python object constructor. Direct record construction is an unsafe test surface outside the versioned scenario execution contract.

claims.statement: A transport redelivery with the same occurrence identity and canonical payload is idempotent across human-state updates and observation or evidence ledgers.

failure_condition.description: A second delivery of the same occurrence and canonical payload applies a second descriptive update, appends a second observation artifact, or strengthens evidence as an independent observation.

claims.statement: Reusing an occurrence identity with a different canonical payload is a hard identity collision and cannot be silently accepted as either a redelivery or a new occurrence.

failure_condition.description: Two different canonical payloads carrying the same occurrence_id are both accepted, one silently overwrites the other, or the second is treated as an ordinary duplicate.

claims.statement: Delayed availability or processing preserves the source occurrence time instead of rewriting a backlog item as a newly occurred event.

failure_condition.description: Processing a delayed delivery replaces its original occurred_at with available_at, processed_at, or the current sim_time.

claims.statement: An explicit current reexposure is distinct from transport redelivery: it may produce a new descriptive update while preserving a reference to the past source and cannot automatically strengthen the source EvidenceLink.

failure_condition.description: An explicitly identified current reexposure is collapsed into transport duplication solely because it references a past source, rewrites the past occurred_at, or creates independent source evidence without a new grounding observation.

claims.notes: The structural test demonstrates one legal descriptive update; it does not establish how human reexposure generally behaves.

claims.statement: The Q-v1 derived ledger contains exactly one qualification receipt per processed occurrence, its mental transitions are exactly the qualified receipt subset, and transport duplicates or redundant deliveries create neither an extra receipt nor a transition.

failure_condition.description: A processed occurrence has zero or multiple Q receipts, a qualified receipt lacks its mental transition, an unqualified receipt creates one, or a transport duplicate or redundant delivery adds either record.

claims.statement: Mental-transition qualification is an immutable post-run derived projection: changing Q-v1 threshold or field scope may change only the mental-transition ledger and cannot change the base HumanState trajectory, processing trace, evidence records, or action records.

failure_condition.description: Holding the scenario and non-Q engine configuration fixed while changing only Q policy changes a non-mental-transition state or ledger, or a derived report is fed back into the run that generated it.

claims.notes: Read-only means no feedback in the implemented run path; it does not prohibit a future separately specified experiment from consuming a prior report.

claims.statement: Q-v1 uses only current-checkpoint trace information, assigns transition_effective_at and qualified_at to processed_at, and later events cannot requalify an unchanged processing-trace prefix.

failure_condition.description: Appending later events changes prior receipts or transitions for an identical processing prefix, or a qualified transition is assigned to source occurrence or availability time rather than processed_at.

claims.statement: MORPH-001A reducer-proposal instrumentation records exactly one occurrence-lineage-bound and operator-schema-bound receipt per processed occurrence from current reducer information, remains independent of Q qualification, preserves traced/compatibility committed-state equality, and does not supply proposal artifacts as inputs to state, routing, evidence, or action updates.

failure_condition.description: A processed occurrence has zero or multiple proposal receipts, a transport redelivery adds a receipt, receipt content changes without changing content identity, inconsistent occurrence lineage is accepted, a later event rewrites an identical receipt prefix, proposals are emitted only for Q-qualified transitions, traced and compatibility reducers commit different states, proposal capture converts the existing finite-invalid-state audit path into an exception, or proposal artifacts become downstream update inputs.

claims.notes: Current reducer information includes stage/write-local pre-constraint and committed targets plus typed driver contributions. Driver channels are an algebraic decomposition under a fixed operator schema, not independently reproduced source causality; constructor validation proves structural admissibility while the inline reducer builder is the trusted writer for evaluated values. Content-bound IDs and state chains enforce internal schema consistency under the canonical builder; they are not cryptographic proof of external source truth. This invariant establishes trace integrity only; ReducerProposal is not an independently identified DeformationDemand.

claims.statement: MORPH-001B is an opt-in immutable post-run projection with one source-bound comparison receipt per ReducerProposalReceipt and one ordered component per source proposal; changing its declared band policy cannot change the source proposal ledger or the generating state, routing, evidence, action, or Q-v1 trajectory.

failure_condition.description: A source proposal receipt or ordered write is missing, duplicated, reordered, or spliced in the comparison ledger; transport redelivery adds a comparison; reexposure or later-prefix provenance changes; policy variation changes the generating execution or source proposal ledger; or the opt-in comparison changes the existing finite-invalid-state audit path.

claims.notes: Source IDs, proposal and state digests, snapshot identity, policy digest, comparison digest, and exact source-ledger validation provide internal consistency under the canonical builder. They are not electronic signatures or proof that a directly constructed self-consistent synthetic record describes an external fact.

claims.statement: Within any future INTERP detached lab, ReceptionState, TargetForm readouts, subjective encounter proxies, private traces, Ghost candidates, assembly candidates, adjudication receipts, and Episode integration artifacts may influence simulated access or interpretation but cannot create or strengthen EvidenceLink, certify source or target identity, rewrite a past occurrence or first-encounter receipt, or bypass a separate Narrative writer.

failure_condition.description: Changing an INTERP reception, target-form, Ghost, assembly, adjudication, or integration fixture changes external evidence strength, certifies an unresolved source or target identity, mutates a past source or encounter receipt, creates a world occurrence, or performs a Narrative write without its separately declared writer path.

claims.notes: INTERP-001B implements this boundary only for the frozen M1 reception/assembly/adjudication slice through detached projections and package/API isolation. TargetForm, variable Ghost exploration, private-trace residence, Dynamics integration, and human validity remain unimplemented.

claims.statement: A future INTERP access occurrence at processing or access ordinal k must form its encounter from pre-access ReceptionState and TargetForm snapshots; artifacts derived at k cannot affect formation of that same encounter and may become effective only at a strictly later access ordinal, while future events cannot rewrite the unchanged receipt prefix.

failure_condition.description: An encounter reads ReceptionState or TargetForm output produced by its own access ordinal, effective order is inferred only from a wall-clock timestamp despite same-time sequencing, or adding a later access or event changes an already emitted prefix artifact.

claims.notes: Multiple access occurrences may share a canonical timestamp; processing_sequence or access_ordinal, not wall-clock time alone, defines the causal order. INTERP-001D1 now executes and evaluates its finite cell-local no-consumption contract: source, formation and Ghost-path cells use fresh detached ledgers, emit no TargetForm revision or feedback, and pass the frozen static no-back-edge and declared effective-order checks. It still does not implement a later-access temporal extension sequence, writer, retention or future-extension replay; full prefix stability remains an INTERP-001D2 gate. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001A registers real-world-domain, author-origin hypothetical functional-jurisdiction scenarios and non-exclusive registered candidate probe domains before D2. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001B freezes a prefix/initial/one-future ordinal trace-definition contract, applies the same registered future option to both compared prior paths, and preserves unexpected effects in an explicit out-of-model lane. It does not execute the later access, node clamp, writer, retention or future-extension replay. Its candidate surface is not an observation, natural earliest divergence is not direct causal residence, and durable TargetForm versus slow cache remains not identifiable under the one-future-access horizon. A/B schema and semantic validation are not later-access trace implementations or support results. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001P0-v0 now freezes a separate elicitation E/R schedule in which a future acquisition protocol must log prompt delivery separately and R1/R2 may become mapping candidates for O5/O10. Its current runner only materializes SCRIPTED_ADVERSARIAL_RESPONSE replay records and emits no actual delivery or response occurrence. It executes no k-to-k+1 internal recurrence, future-extension replay, writer or retention, and its validation is not later-access support. This broad claim therefore stays UNIMPLEMENTED with structural support empty; the D1 report is a target-scoped synthetic artifact, while the dialogue artifacts are author-origin hypothetical and unexecuted method contracts, and none supports later-access causality.

claims.statement: Canonical elapsed time, per-processed-occurrence qualification receipt, qualified mental transition, qualified count, and qualified density are distinct typed quantities or records and cannot be freely cast into one another.

failure_condition.description: A canonical schema aliases processed receipt, qualified transition, elapsed duration, qualified count, and density; uses transport deliveries as transitions; or permits an unrecorded cast between these quantities.

claims.notes: IMPLEMENTED is limited to Q-v1 simulation types and derived reports. It does not adopt HM-DYN-001 or identify a natural unit of consciousness.

claims.statement: Independently identified pre-transition DeformationDemand, typed AccommodationEnvelope or CapacityProfile, ExcessDemand, CommittedDeformation, UncommittedResidual, MorphicLoadProfile, TraceCandidate, and horizon-qualified PersistentTrace are distinct quantities or records and cannot be freely cast into one another.

failure_condition.description: A canonical schema defines demand from post-update committed change, aliases demand with commit, treats the storage bound as an accommodation envelope, labels the arithmetic demand-commit gap as residual strain without a separate operator, applies capacity both inside demand and again inside load, or records an immediate TraceCandidate as PersistentTrace without a retention horizon.

claims.notes: HM-MEAS-003 instruments the current reducer's pre-constraint proposal, but that proxy already includes model-specific drivers and does not implement this capacity-independent demand/load type claim. This type hygiene is not support for the existence or predictive value of morphic load.

claims.statement: MorphicLoadProfile, a future PhenomenalStrainReadout, a phenomenal report, and external EvidenceLink are distinct records with no automatic cast between them.

failure_condition.description: A schema aliases load, phenomenal readout, report, and evidence or permits one of them to instantiate another without an explicit typed bridge and the required certification provenance.

claims.notes: The separation is adopted; the phenomenal bridge itself remains HOLD.

claims.statement: Source occurrence time, protocol availability time, engine processing time, and the engine simulation-time frontier are distinct temporal fields with different writers.

failure_condition.description: A canonical event or processing trace aliases occurred_at, available_at, processed_at, and sim_time into one untyped timestamp or gives the protocol input authority to pre-certify processed_at.

claims.notes: Implemented status is limited to timestamp schema, writer ownership, and trace preservation. FlowUpdate and EventJump are not part of this claim.

claims.statement: Occurrence identity, transport delivery identity, and an explicit current-reexposure reference are distinct provenance roles and cannot be freely substituted for one another.

failure_condition.description: A schema uses delivery_id as source occurrence identity, treats every new delivery as a new occurrence, or represents current reexposure only by duplicating the original occurrence.

claims.notes: The type split is a protocol provenance contract and makes no empirical claim about human event segmentation.

claims.statement: A stage/write-local pre-constraint ReducerProposal, its bounded committed target, and an occurrence-level net state delta are distinct simulation trace quantities and cannot be freely substituted for one another.

failure_condition.description: A canonical trace reconstructs the requested target from final after-minus-before change, aliases requested and committed targets, loses ordered repeated writes to the same field, or casts a reducer proposal into DeformationDemand, MorphicLoad, qualia, or subjective time.

claims.notes: The operational type split is implemented by ReducerDriverContribution and ReducerFieldProposal, with ReducerStepResult used only as a traced-reducer runtime wrapper. TickTrace and ReducerProposalReceipt/Ledger retain one flat occurrence-wide ordered proposals tuple under descriptive-reducer-preclamp-proxy@1.0.0. ReducerProposalContext binds encoded soothing and performance identity so conditional action/soothing writes and drivers must match the runtime context.

claims.statement: A versioned experimenter-declared ReducerProposalEnvelopePolicy, an ordered ReducerProposal, a ReducerProposalEnvelopeComparison and its ordered proxy profile, and the reducer state-storage constraint are distinct simulation artifacts and cannot be freely substituted for one another.

failure_condition.description: A canonical object aliases the declared simulation band with the storage bound or human accommodation capacity, derives proxy excess from committed clamp loss, loses ordered proposal identity, or freely casts the comparison profile into DeformationDemand, ExcessDemand, ResidualStrain, MorphicLoad, qualia, or mental time.

claims.notes: The code-level policy name denotes an explicit reducer-write simulation parameter. It does not implement the broader human-model AccommodationEnvelope or CapacityProfile in HM-TYPE-002.

claims.statement: A source occurrence, ObservationEvent or EvidenceLink, RFC-0002 current reexposure, detached CurrentAccessOccurrence, experimenter-defined SubjectiveEncounterFormProxy, private experience trace, and EpisodeMaterialReference are distinct candidate records or roles and cannot be freely cast into one another.

failure_condition.description: A canonical construction aliases protocol redelivery or current reexposure with detached current access, casts a subjective encounter or private trace into external evidence, invents unknown source identity or time, or treats an EpisodeMaterialReference as the source occurrence itself.

claims.notes: Adoption fixes the separation requirement only. All new INTERP artifacts remain unimplemented simulation candidates; SubjectiveEncounterFormProxy is not qualia, a first-person report, or a human measurement.

claims.statement: An assembly-independent EpisodeMaterialReference, AssemblyMaterialMembershipCandidate, EpisodeAssemblyCandidate, immutable InterpretiveBindingCandidate, append-only BindingAdjudicationReceipt, optional EpisodeIntegrationReceipt, authored Narrative Field write, and TargetFormReadout are distinct candidate artifacts or transitions and cannot be freely cast into one another.

failure_condition.description: An EpisodeMaterialReference depends on assembly identity while that assembly depends on the same material reference, membership is stored by mutating material, a candidate is mutated into its adjudication outcome, a rejected or deferred candidate is treated as a persistent binding, Ghost writes integration directly, Episode integration automatically writes Narrative, or TargetFormReadout is cast into an external target property or persistent state without a separate writer contract.

claims.notes: Checkpoint 05-A's bare EpisodeMaterial/EpisodeAssembly/NarrativeBinding labels were synthesis candidates; RFC 0004 does not adopt those bare labels as implemented types or as one stable ontology recovered from every historical epoch. Authored Narrative gates do not eliminate endorsement-free implicit formation candidates.

claims.statement: Preregistered qualified mental-transition density predicts held-out descriptive trajectories beyond canonical elapsed time, occurrence identity, and simpler event-count alternatives.

failure_condition.description: Under preregistered qualification and held-out comparisons, transition density adds no predictive or explanatory discrimination beyond canonical elapsed time, occurrence identity, and simpler event-count alternatives.

claims.notes: HM-DEFECT-EVENT-COUNT-SLOW-UPDATE-V01 motivates separating time from event count but does not support this predictive human hypothesis.

claims.statement: At matched canonical time and qualified transition count, preregistered DeformationDemand and CapacityProfile relations predict held-out recovery, residual strain, or horizon-qualified trace retention better than a count-only model after complexity penalties.

failure_condition.description: Across preregistered count-load dissociation and capacity-relative tests, typed demand and capacity add no held-out discrimination over a simpler transition-count model after complexity penalties.

claims.statement: In manifest-frozen matched synthetic fixtures with external evidence held fixed, separately declared ReceptionState access and candidate-coherence profiles can produce different material-access subset or order, assembly, or adjudication capability patterns, including delayed participation of a preserved material reference after a later access occurrence, beyond a state-independent baseline.

failure_condition.description: After exact manifest freeze, R0 through R3 cannot produce the declared access-versus-coherence distinctions under matched evidence and exact-access-set controls, the apparent effect is only an expected-output label or scalar threshold alias, or a reception change without a current access occurrence rewrites past material.

claims.notes: INTERP-001B executes the frozen M1 contract with R0 state-independent, R1 access-only, R2 coherence-only, and factorized R3 over 16 mirrored fixtures and 64 isolated model cells. All 88 signatures and 54 non-retirement assertions conform in the versioned synthetic report, and five retirement rules are not triggered. Implementation remains PARTIAL because the preregistered single-threshold alias rule is NOT_EVALUABLE without its absent baseline, while the HM-INV-013 dependency remains UNIMPLEMENTED beyond a structural no-feedback reception/prefix probe in this frozen slice. TargetForm, Ghost variation, state-dependent encounter formation, broader I/TF/EF manifests, prediction, and human support remain open.

claims.statement: At matched source, evidence, material count and component magnitudes, future manifest-frozen assembly topology, an object-scoped prior TargetForm readout, and Ghost exploration path can create structural adjudication distinctions not reproduced by simple sums, fixed thresholds, or reception-only alternatives, subject to complexity and held-out evaluation before any predictive claim.

failure_condition.description: After exact manifest freeze, I3 or I4 makes no declared distinction beyond simpler count, threshold, access-only, or reception-only models; bridge or Ghost paths are selected after observing outcomes; TargetForm differences exist only through unproven direct answer injection; or future hidden-oracle and held-out evaluation shows no complexity-adjusted benefit.

claims.notes: INTERP-001D1 executed and evaluated three isolated synthetic blocks: outcome-blind TF0-TF2 source compilation, supplied E0/ER/ET/ERT ReceptionState/TargetForm encounter formation, and exact-access G0/GT/GP/GTP Ghost-path ablation. The frozen report passes 88/88 signatures and 91/91 non-retirement assertions. The two triggered RT conditions are one algebraic equivalence family plus its lookup alias control, not two independent challenger reductions or factor retirement. These blocks do not pass outputs downstream, and evaluator-only development/sealed membership is not a prediction split. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001A moves the next question to selective functional jurisdiction in real-world-domain, author-origin hypothetical scenario families; simple sums, thresholds and lookups remain alias or degeneracy controls rather than an explanatory-performance scoreboard. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001B freezes nine factor-level competing placement signatures, three same-future-option oracles, a REL initial-by-later reported-mood factorial, three unexecuted D2a-only node-clamp challenges, and strict alias, identifiability and out-of-model rules. These are conditional hypothesis definitions, not observed human answers or a placement winner. Natural earliest divergence cannot certify direct residence; observed equality is empirically unresolved rather than operational alias; one later access cannot distinguish durable TargetForm from slow cache; and Ghost, adjudicator and action-gate separation has no selective 001A natural intervention. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001P0-v0 freezes a presentation and prompt/response schedule plus exact scripted replay provenance while forbidding the runner from emitting actual participant/model occurrences, mapping responses, classifying OUT_OF_MODEL, issuing defects or selecting placements. Author walkthrough and language inspection remain evaluator-side defect sources rather than response sources. It contains no participant result, frozen response-to-trace mapping, held-out comparison or mechanism adjudication. A/B/P0 registration and validation do not implement recursive D2a, human judgment, LLM activation or a Narrative writer. Supplied-input sensitivity is not Episode/Narrative source support, source-compiler distinction is not downstream necessity, and finite or frozen conformance is not a human mechanism or complexity-adjusted held-out benefit. The broad implementation status therefore remains UNIMPLEMENTED and structural support stays empty. Ghost may broaden, rehearse, moderate, reinforce, polarize, or leave candidates contested; no direction is its universal objective.

claims.statement: A morphic-load measurement must define units, normalization, and missingness rules; estimate typed DeformationDemand from pre-transition shape and incoming constraint; separately derive MorphicLoadProfile from demand and pre-transition CapacityProfile; record achieved work and residual strain; and withhold PersistentTrace status until a preregistered retention horizon.

failure_condition.description: Demand cannot be estimated before the modeled update, demand and capacity contributions to load are not separately identifiable, achieved work cannot be distinguished from residual strain, or trace retention cannot be calibrated at the preregistered horizon.

claims.notes: MORPH-001A supplies reducer proposal/commit instrumentation and MORPH-001B supplies an experimenter-declared simulation-band proxy comparison. Neither identifies DeformationDemand or measured human AccommodationEnvelope, and they do not satisfy this load measurement claim because residual, recovery, retention, calibration, and separate demand/capacity identification remain undefined.

claims.statement: Q-v1 is a simulation measurement model that evaluates one processed-occurrence checkpoint at a time over versioned literal persistent descriptive fields, records typed before/after deltas, qualifies a receipt when at least one absolute delta reaches 0.01 normalized_simulation_unit by default, and reports qualified count and density as distinct typed quantities.

failure_condition.description: The declared policy cannot reproduce its typed deltas or qualification from the current checkpoint trace, threshold or field-scope changes are not identified by the policy digest, undeclared fields enter the scope, or count, duration, and density cannot be recovered as distinct report quantities.

claims.notes: Calibration here means internal reproducibility and identifiability in normalized simulation units. No human threshold, natural mental-time unit, or predictive advantage has been calibrated. The default scope includes access fields still influenced by legacy_v01_access_pressure_bridge; excluding the queue object does not remove that indirect confound. Per-processed-occurrence Q-v1 also does not guarantee stability under unresolved same-time ordering or alternative semantic bundling. Queue/access decoupling or scope ablation and a separate serialization failure probe are required before HM-DYN-001 evaluation.

claims.statement: MORPH-001A is a simulation instrumentation model that preserves one flat occurrence-wide ordered tuple of stage/write-qualified pre-constraint reducer targets, bounded committed targets, and typed driver contributions in one ReducerProposalReceipt per processed occurrence.

failure_condition.description: The trace cannot identify the versioned writer and stage that produced a proposal, reconstruct requested and committed field deltas in normalized simulation units, reproduce their dissociation under a preregistered saturation control, or keep the record distinct from Q qualification and future envelope/load quantities.

claims.notes: The measurement identity is descriptive-reducer-preclamp-proxy@1.0.0. ReducerProposal is not DeformationDemand: the proposal can already include model-specific access pressure, update rate, phenomenal activation, performance, and other current drivers. Passing structural probes supplies no support for HM-DYN-002.

claims.statement: MORPH-001B is a simulation-only measurement model that compares each MORPH-001A signed requested_delta with an explicit asymmetric per-field declared policy band using componentwise clipping and preserves the ordered signed result as a source- and policy-identified proxy profile.

failure_condition.description: The declared field ranges, unit, source proposal identity, componentwise operator, or ordered write mapping cannot reproduce the proxy profile; storage clamp loss substitutes for the band comparison; direction is lost; repeated writes collapse; or a policy change is not identified by policy and receipt identity.

claims.notes: The measurement identity is reducer-proposal-envelope-comparison@1.0.0. Policy bands are synthetic author-declared parameters. Internal reproducibility does not calibrate human accommodation, identify DeformationDemand, define ExcessDemand or MorphicLoad, or support HM-DYN-002.

claims.statement: Any future ReceptionStateSnapshot, SubjectiveEncounterFormProxy, or TargetFormReadout measurement model must declare non-cancelling component axes, scale or units, missingness, source and writer, interpreted-target and external-entity resolution, pre-access snapshot and effective ordinal, available information, model and policy digest, and calibration status before emitting comparable receipts.

failure_condition.description: A proxy omits axes, units or declared scale, missingness, writer, source resolution, pre-access snapshot, effective ordinal, policy digest, or calibration status; cancels positive and negative components into an unvalidated scalar; or is interpreted as actual qualia, measured mood, external identity, or human-calibrated TargetForm.

claims.notes: INTERP-001B executes only the integer ordinal rank 0/1/2, explicit missingness, non-cancellation and frozen manifest/contract/access-ordinal-bound reception/encounter proxy slice. It does not emit an HM-MEAS-005-complete comparable receipt: source/writer, pre-access snapshot receipt, available-information, model/policy receipt identity, interpreted-target/external-entity mapping, and calibration requirements are not all carried by one output. INTERP-DIALOGUE-001P0-v0 is explicitly a development elicitation instrument: its current runner records only scripted replay schedule/status/payload provenance, not actual delivery or response occurrences, and it has no frozen response-to-internal-trace mapping, calibration or comparable human receipt. Actual acquisition requires a later source-specific pre-run freeze. Its contract validation adds no HM-MEAS-005 support. TargetForm measurement and comparable human measurement remain unimplemented, and synthetic reproducibility does not identify a human latent variable.

claims.statement: The slime-shape image is an explanatory metaphor for current form, admissible deformation range, incoming constraint, required reformation, and residual strain.

failure_condition.description: The slime metaphor is cited as empirical or physical evidence, used to supply an unmeasured unit, or treated as proof of the morphic-load hypothesis.
